channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: Precision therapeutics for wound healing: Molecular targets, signalling networks, and advanced drug design strategies
item.description: This review examines precision therapeutic approaches for wound healing, focusing on molecular targets and signalling networks that drive the healing process. The article outlines advanced drug design strategies that leverage these mechanistic insights to develop targeted interventions for wound rep

item.title: Small molecular therapeutic targets for neurodegenerative diseases
item.description: This review examines small molecule therapeutic targets relevant to neurodegenerative diseases, drawing on current research in neurochemistry and biotechnology to identify molecular pathways and compounds of interest for drug development in this therapeutic area.

item.title: Recent strategies and approaches for the development of molecularly targeted nanomedicine
item.description: This review examines current strategies and approaches for developing molecularly targeted nanomedicines, which represent an advancing field in drug delivery technology designed to improve therapeutic specificity and efficacy.

item.title: OpenAI researcher Miles Wang in talks to launch AI drug discovery startup valued at $2B
item.description: The funding discussions point to investor interest in applying AI to make breakthroughs in life sciences.

item.title: American-hellenic chamber: corporate governance key to market credibility
item.description: Corporate governance should strengthen investor confidence and corporate competitiveness by focusing less on bureaucracy and more on substance, speakers said at the 12th Corporate Governance Conference organised by the American-Hellenic… American-Hellenic Cha…

item.title: Already rich, already successful, why the last wave of tech winners is grinding again | techcrunch
item.description: They're rolling up their sleeves again, seemingly out of fear of missing AI's defining moment and, presumably, the irresistible allure of making even more money -- potentially a lot more.

item.title: Gobi x: creating more energy for AI, not taking it from society
item.description: PARTNER CONTENT: How Envision is reversing the datacenter playbook by making computing chase abundant desert power, not the other way around

item.title: Pharmaceutical Engineering of Lipid-Based Nanocarriers for Cancer Nanomedicines: From Doxorubicin to mRNA Platforms
item.description: This review examines the pharmaceutical engineering of lipid-based nanocarriers for cancer treatment, covering applications ranging from conventional chemotherapy delivery (doxorubicin) to emerging mRNA-based therapeutic platforms. Lipid nanocarriers represent a critical drug delivery technology tha

item.title: OpenAI’s first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker that can move
item.description: The device is weirdly described as involving "mechanical elements that can move on their own" and the Bloomberg report includes the detail that the device is designed to "feel like a companion and become a physical manifestation of OpenAI’s ChatGPT."

item.title: OpenAI’s new flagship model deletes files on its own, people keep warning
item.description: A number of social media posts claim that GPT-5.6 Sol deleted files and data without warning. OpenAI had basically disclosed the problem in June.

item.title: Spotify expands its AI push with a ChatGPT-like music assistant
item.description: Spotify is rolling out a new AI-powered conversational feature that lets Premium subscribers chat with the app to discover music, podcasts, audiobooks, and more.

item.title: The Download: Claude’s inner workings, and the future of world models
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show —James O’Donnell When Anthropic announced last week that it had found a new window in

item.title: Author correction: the stard-ai reporting guideline for diagnostic accuracy studies using artificial intelligence
item.description: Nature Medicine - Author Correction: The STARD-AI reporting guideline for diagnostic accuracy studies using artificial intelligence

item.title: Big-time AI law kicking into gear requiring chatbots to tell you they are AI, but might not move the needle
item.description: The EU AI Act provision on AI disclosure kicks into gear on August 2, 2026. It's a big deal. Will it be useful or not? An AI Insider analysis and scoop.

item.title: Ranking: Who are the insurance industry’s AI talent, maturity leaders?
item.description: One out of every 50 employees working at a group of 30 large insurers is an AI specialist, according to an analysis published by a benchmarking firm, which ranks Allianz, AXA and Chubb as AI talent leaders. For London-based Evident, …

item.title: “learn to love maha” vs. “how to make the cdc great again”?
item.description: MAHA apologist Dr. Joseph Marine recently wrote an article on how to make the CDC great again. Wait, what? Trump's been in office a year and a half, and the CDC isn't great? The post “Learn to love MAHA” vs. “how to make the CDC great again”? first appeared o…

item.title: Ai-powered digital marketing: the cybersecurity blind spot every marketer must fix + video - undercode testing
item.description: AI-Powered Digital Marketing: The Cybersecurity Blind Spot Every Marketer Must Fix + Video - "Undercode Testing": Monitor hackers like a pro. Get real-time

item.title: Dietary intervention and bmi reduction in individuals at the extremes of genetic predisposition to higher bmi: a randomized controlled trial
item.description: In the GENEROOS randomized controlled trial, adults at the extremes of BMI polygenic score responded equally to a six-month dietary intervention. Genetic predisposition to obesity did not modify weight loss.

item.title: Pervasive interactions between exposures and polygenic risk can inform more effective clinical and behavioral interventions
item.description: This study examines interactions between polygenic scores and pairs of environmental contexts across seven diseases and introduces the proportion needed to benefit as a metric to quantify the expected effectiveness of interventions as a function of polygenic …

item.title: Meta admits its first ‘superintelligence’ was too stupid to survive for three days
item.description: Pulls AI-powered image tweaker after allowing free-for-all

item.title: Lenovo denies using banned chinese ssds where they're not allowed
item.description: PLUS: Canadian/Bhutanese datacenter for India; China re-uses a rocket; Australia signals AI intervention; And more!

item.title: Anthropic starts localizing Claude pricing for India, its biggest market after the US
item.description: Claude users in India are starting to see Indian rupee-denominated subscription plans.

item.title: Waze adds new AI-powered features and customization updates
item.description: Some of the new features are powered by Google's Gemini AI assistant, which reflects the tech giant's broader push to integrate Gemini across its products while also better positioning Waze to compete with rival services such as Apple Maps.

item.title: What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Anthropic—currently the world’s most valuable AI company, with a nearly $1 trillion valuation—has a reputation for publishing strange and heady research. It’s loo

item.title: Autonomous AI in healthcare: innovation’s promise, patient safety’s challenge
item.description: AI in healthcare: The central issue is not whether autonomous AI should be used in healthcare. It is whether healthcare systems can develop the governance, validation, transparency, cybersecurity, and accountability mechanisms necessary to deploy such technol…

item.title: Structural requirements of blood factors binding to soluble hexon trimers with implications for adenovirus cell targeting and immune evasion
item.description: Author summary Viruses have evolved ingenious ways of masking themselves to evade detection and clearance by the host immune system. In the bloodstream, species-C human adenoviruses (HAdVs) coat themselves with the coagulation factors to avoid identification …

item.title: Cognitive “black boxes”: a philosophical examination of non-human minds and ai-driven science
item.description: Zakharova, D. (2026). Cognitive “black boxes”: a philosophical examination of non-human minds and AI-driven science [PhD thesis]. London School of Economics and Political Science. https://doi.org/10.21953/researchonline.lse.ac.uk.00140240

item.title: Red blood cell distribution width to albumin ratio and systemic immune-inflammatory index as predictors of mortality in severe pneumonia: a retrospective cohort analysis
item.description: Objective Severe pneumonia carries a high risk of mortality. There is a need for readily available prognostic biomarkers to improve risk stratification. This study evaluated the prognostic value of two novel composite indices, the red blood cell distribution …

item.title: New compliance guidance available: hitrust i1 on aws
item.description: We are pleased to announce the publication of a new AWS compliance implementation guidance: HITRUST i1 Compliance on AWS: Customer Implementation Guidance with an Illustrative Healthcare Platform. Healthcare organizations seeking HITRUST i1 certification incr…

item.title: Meta files patent for AI that can listen all day and track how you're feeling
item.description: Meta has filed a patent application for an AI that listens to your voice throughout the day, works out how it thinks you are feeling from the way you sound, and keeps a timestamped log of every read. Each read gets pinned to the moment it happened: the time,…

item.title: The new way practices are turning happy patients into marketing — without the awkward camera setup
item.description: IntroductionPatient reviews have long been a critical element of health care decisions. In the plastic surgery field, which is very personal, what patients have to say about their experiences with the treatments, the recovery, and the overall satisfaction is …

item.title: Psychiatry lacks biomarkers. can this eeg ballcap get a base hit?
item.description: Universal Brain's EEG cap seeks to bring precision into mental health treatment and drug development. With $7.9 million raised, they are entering a fast-moving landscape.

item.title: Ull and imetisa create health chairs in telemedicine and neurocomputa…
item.description: The University of La Laguna and the public company Imetisa are launching two new chairs to enhance research and teaching in diagnostic imaging, neurocomputation, and telemedicine.

item.title: A scoping review on emerging biomarkers in inflammatory bowel disease: towards precision medicine in diagnosis and therapeutic management
item.description: Background Inflammatory Bowel Diseases (IBD) are chronic conditions presenting significant diagnostic and management challenges. Current invasive methods and traditional biomarkers often lack sufficient accuracy and fail to address the disease’s heterogeneity…

item.title: Multi-cloud lakehouse architecture on aws for agentic AI, part 1: architecture and best practices
item.description: This post focuses on explaining the architecture approach to build the open lakehouse architecture on AWS, unifying the metadata catalog across providers for the AI agents to access. In addition, it highlights the architecture trade-offs and best practices.

item.title: The AI revolution: innovation, cybersecurity, and societal prospects
item.description: Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming global economies, governments, and daily life in an "acceleration era." AI is also broadening the cyber risk landscape.

item.title: Self-compassion, adverse childhood experiences and perceived stress on college students’ mental health: the role of resilience
item.description: Background The college environment presents various academic and life pressures that affect students’ mental health. Previous studies have examined self-compassion, perceived stress, and adverse childhood experiences separately, but few have integrated them w…

item.title: Self-explaining artificial intelligence for the classification of b cell non-hodgkin lymphoma: a diagnostic decision support study
item.description: In this diagnostic decision support study, Michael C. Thrun and colleagues develop and validate Self-explaining artificial intelligence system (FlowXAI) for the classification of B cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma.

item.title: Comparison of moducare versus wait-and-see approach for histologically proven low-grade cervical intraepithelial neoplasia (cin1) (moducin1 trial) – study protocol
item.description: Human Papillomavirus (HPV) is causally associated with cervical cancer and precancerous lesions (dysplasias) of the cervix. The treatment of choice for low-grade lesions is monitoring with no-treatment, because the majority of them will regress spontaneously.…

item.title: Uncertainty-aware quantitative analysis of high-throughput live cell migration data
item.description: Author summary Cell migration velocity varies across biological processes–from immune responses to cancer metastasis. Yet accurate measurement remains challenging due to inherent cell-to-cell variability and systematic differences between biological condition…

item.title: The impact of pectin supplementation on systemic inflammation pathways, gut microbiome, and metabolic health in patients with metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease (masld): a study protocol for a randomised controlled trial
item.description: Background Metabolic dysfunction–associated steatotic liver disease (MASLD) is the leading cause of chronic liver disease, affecting over 30% of adults worldwide. Emerging evidence suggests that dietary fibre, particularly pectin, may improve metabolic health…

item.title: Genome-wide characterization of copy number variants and their functional relevance in indigenous draught cattle of south asia
item.description: Copy number variations (CNVs) are an important source of structural genomic variation and contribute to phenotypic diversity in cattle, including traits related to production, reproduction, and adaptation. In this study, we performed a genome-wide characteriz…

item.title: Mou 225-26-007
item.description: This Memorandum of Understanding ("MOU") between the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), and the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) (hereafter “the Parties” or “Federal Partners”) provides a f…

item.title: A generalized additive partial-mastery cognitive diagnosis model
item.description: Cardenas Hurtado, C. ORCID logo , Lee, S. M. , Chen, Y. ORCID logo & Moustaki, I. ORCID logo (2026). A generalized additive partial-mastery cognitive diagnosis model. Annals of Applied Statistics, [In Press]

item.title: Zuck's AI ambitions put meta on course to become america's next big cloud provider
item.description: Renting out spare compute is simply the natural progression for any sufficiently large infra company

item.title: Zig creator calls bun’s claude rust rewrite ‘unreviewed slop’
item.description: The port took just 11 days and about $165,000 at API pricing

item.title: Satya nadella has issued a shocking warning to companies using AI | techcrunch
item.description: Of all the debates raging about the potential downsides of AI, there is one worry causing the most hand-wringing among AI enthusiasts in Silicon Valley — that the giant AI labs that sell proprietary models are somehow acting like Trojan horses.

item.title: The wildest allegations in apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against openai | techcrunch
item.description: Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI contains allegations that range from employees joking about unauthorized access to Apple’s systems to claims that job candidates were asked to bring Apple hardware to interviews. Here are the complaint’s most eye-c…

item.title: Microsoft chief turns hostile on frontier AI labs, warns companies to guard their ip
item.description: Lock it down, warns Satya Nadella, seemingly forgetting the billions Redmond chipped in to OpenAI back in the good old days

item.title: Anthropic starts localizing claude pricing for india, its biggest market after the US | techcrunch
item.description: Anthropic starts localizing Claude pricing for India, its biggest market after the US | TechCrunchtechcrunch.com
